channel.title: NASA 新闻 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: NASA 官方新闻 + 英译简

item.title: NASA 邀请媒体观看罗马太空望远镜和 Crew-13 发射
item.description:
NASA 的 Nancy Grace Roman 太空望远镜和该机构的 SpaceX Crew-13 任务现已开放媒体认证，这两项任务都计划在未来几个月内进行发射。
这架罗马望远镜定于美国东部时间 8 月 30 日（星期日）上午 7 点 20 分搭乘 SpaceX 猎鹰重型火箭从美国宇航局佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心的 39A 发射场发射升空。该天文台以美国宇航局第一位首席天文学家的名字命名，将对宇宙进行深入的全景观察，生成前所未见的图片，这将彻底改变我们对宇宙的理解。
Crew-13 计划于 9 月中旬从卡纳维拉尔角太空部队站的 40 号航天发射场发射。该任务将搭载美国宇航局宇航员杰西卡·沃特金斯和卢克·德莱尼、加拿大航天局宇航员约书亚·库特里克和俄罗斯航天局宇航员谢尔盖·捷捷亚特尼科夫前往国际空间站进行科学研究探险。这将是 NASA 使用 SpaceX 的猎鹰 9 号火箭和龙飞船执行的第 13 次商业载人轮换任务。
有兴趣参加其中一项或两项发布会的媒体必须在以下截止日期前提出申请：
所有认证请求必须在线提交：
NASA 的媒体认证政策已上线。有关认证或特殊后勤请求的问题，请发送电子邮件至：ksc-media-accreditat@mail.nasa.gov。
如有其他问题，请联系 NASA 肯尼迪新闻编辑室：321-867-2468。
有关这些任务的更多信息，请访问：
Alise Fisher（罗马）/ Joshua Finch (Crew-13)总部，华盛顿202-385-1287 / 202-358-2546alise.m.fisher@nasa.gov/joshua.a.finch@nasa.gov
Leejay Lockhart（罗马）/ Steve Siceloff（Crew-13）肯尼迪航天中心，佛罗里达州 321-747-8310 / 321-867-2468leejay.lockhart@nasa.gov/steven.p.siceloff@nasa.gov
克莱尔·安德烈奥利（罗马）戈达德太空飞行中心，格林贝尔特，马里兰州 301-286-1940claire.andreoli@nasa.gov

item.title: 美国宇航局的心灵任务在飞越火星期间拍摄了火星表面的细节图像
item.description:
这是由 NASA Psyche 任务中的多光谱成像仪捕获的增强彩色马赛克图像，由 2026 年 5 月 15 日航天器飞越火星期间获取的四张单独图像组成。
在获取该马赛克图像所需的六分钟内，Psyche 从右向左（火星上的东北到西南）行进，像素尺度分辨率从右侧每像素 381 米到左侧每像素 440 米不等。成像仪使用其近红外、绿色和蓝色滤光片，有助于显示表面上对比度极高的火山口、山脊、风纹和火山平原物质。
该马赛克覆盖了火星崎岖的南部高地的 Iapygia 地区的部分区域，从东经 62 度到东经 78 度，北纬 4 度到南纬 14 度。最大的陨石坑位于中心下方，称为福尼尔陨石坑，直径约为 71 英里（114 公里）。中心左侧马赛克从上到下的线性特征是称为 Oenotria Scopuli 的长不规则悬崖（或陡坡）系统的一部分，该系统是该区域东北部大型 Isidis 冲击盆地圆形结构的一部分。
有关 NASA Psyche 任务的更多信息，请访问：

item.title: 美国宇航局欢迎毛里求斯成为第 70 个阿尔忒弥斯协议签署国
item.description:
毛里求斯共和国正式加入致力于负责任太空探索的全球联盟，成为《阿耳忒弥斯协议》的最新签署国和第七个非洲国家。美国宇航局副局长马特·安德森 (Matt Anderson) 为周五在岛国埃贝纳市举行的签字仪式发表了视频讲话。
安德森说：“我们很荣幸欢迎毛里求斯加入阿耳忒弥斯协议社区，并期待在未来几年中共同努力。” “我们正在共同为未来的探索奠定基础，同时确保太空保持和平、方便且造福所有人。美国将重返月球并开启探索和发现的黄金时代。这项工作需要有能力的合作伙伴以及对负责任探索的共同承诺。”
毛里求斯高等教育、科学研究部常务秘书 Navindsing Jugmohunsing 代表该国签署了《阿耳忒弥斯协议》。美国负责非洲事务的副助理国务卿莎拉·特劳特曼和美国驻毛里求斯临时代办克雷格·哈布迈尔出席了签字仪式。
“毛里求斯加入《阿耳忒弥斯协议》标志着我们新太空之旅的决定性篇章，”贾格莫洪辛说。 “作为印度洋的一个小岛屿发展中国家，我们致力于通过保护我们的海洋和海岸线并扩大像我们这样的国家的声音，确保太空为人类服务。毛里求斯随时准备帮助塑造太空治理的未来，同时释放创新和伙伴关系的新机遇。”
由于毛里求斯的战略位置，美国宇航局首先通过其早期的全球测绘工作与毛里求斯进行接触。 1965 年至 1980 年间，美国宇航局 (NASA) 使用多次卫星任务来收集地球大小和形状的全球测量数据。作为这项工作的一部分，美国宇航局派出团队前往毛里求斯和其他支持卫星摄影进行大地测量分析的国际跟踪站。他们的观察加强了从阿波罗到阿尔忒弥斯所使用的导航技术，并为今天《阿尔忒弥斯协议》重申的伙伴关系奠定了基础。
2020 年，美国宇航局和国务院与其他七个创始国共同制定了《阿耳忒弥斯协议》，以回应政府和私营公司对月球活动日益增长的兴趣。他们提出了第一套实用原则，旨在加强志同道合的国家在探索月球、火星及其他地方时的安全和协调，并承诺各国：
五年后，唐纳德·J·特朗普总统的国家太空政策指示美国宇航局建立一个可持续的月球前哨站。通过这个月球基地，美国宇航局正在将《阿耳忒弥斯协议》的原则付诸实践，邀请包括现在的毛里求斯在内的所有签署国参与这一努力。
随着美国宇航局继续努力在太空建立安全、和平和繁荣的未来，预计未来数月乃至数年将有更多国家签署《阿耳忒弥斯协议》。

item.title: 几内亚比绍潮汐推动的生物多样性宝库
item.description:
比热戈斯群岛广阔的泥滩、沙滩和红树林为大量迁徙的滨鸟和大量海龟提供了栖息地。
潮汐每天两次涨落，流经几内亚比绍 Bijagós 群岛（葡萄牙语 Arquipélago dos Bijagós）88 个岛屿和小岛两侧的迷宫般的沙质河道、泥滩和红树林。从上面看，这个过程导致地貌发生了明显的变化：退潮时，潮间带泥滩和沙滩从海上出现，导致岛屿显着增大，几小时后再次缩小。
潮汐的永恒节奏维持着群岛上海洋生物的大量繁殖，截至 2025 年，该群岛已被列入联合国教科文组织世界遗产名录。该遗址保护着非洲大西洋沿岸唯一活跃的三角洲群岛，在这里，潮汐、河流沉积物、沿海上升流和沿海洋流汇聚在一起，形成了异常多产和生物多样性的岛屿生态系统。
联合国教科文组织估计，这些岛屿养育着大约 87 万只候鸟，使这里成为西非东大西洋迁徙路线沿线鸟类最重要的觅食区之一。数百种鸟类以低潮时暴露在泥滩上的海洋蠕虫、甲壳类动物、软体动物和小鱼为食。涨潮时，海牛、海豚和鱼群会靠近岛屿，深入周围的红树林，数以万计的海龟会游到内陆的沙滩上寻找筑巢地。
大量绿海龟在波伊朗小岛上筑巢，该岛是若昂维埃拉和波伊朗海洋国家公园的一部分。孵化后，幼龟会在夜间危险地冲入水中，经常受到螃蟹、蜥蜴和鸟类的追赶。一旦到达水中，小海龟就会面临一系列捕食者，包括在浅水区巡逻的鲹鱼、梭鱼、石斑鱼和笛鲷，以及在较深水域巡逻的金枪鱼、鲭鱼、鲨鱼和鳐鱼。据估计，只有不到 1% 的绿海龟幼龟能活到成年。
2025 年对该地区潮汐的分析探讨了为什么该群岛拥有西非最大的潮汐范围。研究人员得出结论，该地区宽而浅的陆架和河口的几何形状相结合，产生了高达 7 米（23 英尺）的潮差，而西非海岸许多其他地区的潮差约为 1 米（3 英尺）。科学家们使用来自 NASA/CNES TOPEX/Poseidon、Jason-1 和 Jason-2 卫星的测高数据来帮助验证他们的发现。
NASA 地球观测站图像由 Lauren Dauphin 使用来自美国的陆地卫星数据拍摄地质调查。 亚当·沃伊兰的故事。
当我们探索宇宙并发现更多关于我们的家园星球时，请随时了解 NASA 的最新内容。
水下喷发产生的浮力火山岩碎片漂流过俾斯麦海并堵塞了岛屿海岸。
不列颠哥伦比亚省温哥华岛周围的鲱鱼活动使沿海水域变得明亮，足以从太空中观测到。
海狸岛是密歇根湖北部群岛上一串青翠美丽的宝石中的一个。
订阅地球观测站并将地球放入您的收件箱中。
美国宇航局地球观测站每天为您带来地球的深入故事和令人惊叹的图像。
开放访问 NASA 的地球科学数据档案

item.title: 美国宇航局欢迎塞尔维亚成为最新的阿尔忒弥斯协议签署国
item.description:
周四，塞尔维亚共和国在 NASA 在华盛顿总部举办的仪式上签署了《阿耳忒弥斯协议》，成为第 69 个加入致力于和平、透明和负责任的太空探索的志同道合国家大家庭的国家。
“塞尔维亚与美国宇航局的联系可以追溯到阿波罗计划，当时塞尔维亚工程师的工作帮助人类在太空中取得了一些最伟大的成就，”美国宇航局副局长马特·安德森说。 “其中包括 Milojko ‘Mike’ Vučelić，他因在阿波罗 13 号宇航员安全回家中发挥的关键作用而被授予总统自由勋章。他们的故事提醒我们，太空中最伟大的成就是通过才华横溢的人们的共同努力实现的。”
在阿波罗时代，更广泛的塞尔维亚裔美国工程师团队在系统工程、推进、电力系统、航天器对接、电子可靠性和任务协调方面发挥了关键作用。他们的专业知识支持从月球着陆分析到安全航天器对接等关键功能。
塞尔维亚外交部长马尔科·杜里奇代表塞尔维亚签署了《阿尔忒弥斯协议》。
“伟大的超越始终激励着人类实现最伟大的壮举——从罗马人的‘per aspera ad astra’到诺曼·文森特·皮尔的信念，即如果我们瞄准月球，我们至少会降落在群星之中，”杜里奇说。 “这些话在今天感觉特别合适。我们来自一个拥有像尼古拉·特斯拉和米卢廷·米兰科维奇这样伟大思想的国家，也来自大卫·胡伊奇的遗产，他是阿波罗任务的先驱之一，也是‘塞尔维亚七人组’的成员，这是一群工程师和技术人员，他们为美国宇航局做出了贡献，使登月成为可能。本着这种精神，我们应该感谢我们勇敢的祖先和我们的孩子，不断推动新的领域——探索、互相激励、并敢于做更伟大的事情。”
通过签署《阿尔忒弥斯协议》，各国为美国宇航局未来月球探索的机会打开了大门，例如为美国主导的月球基地和立方体卫星提供科学和技术有效载荷，以执行即将到来的阿尔忒弥斯任务，推动人类重返月球，并塑造太空探索和创新的黄金时代。
塞尔维亚共和国驻美国大使德拉甘·苏塔诺瓦茨；塞尔维亚科学、技术发展和创新部国务秘书 Marija Gnjatović；以及美国国务院负责海洋和国际环境与科学事务的助理国务卿韦斯利·布鲁克斯都参加了塞尔维亚的签字仪式。
2020 年，美国宇航局和国务院与其他七个创始国共同制定了《阿耳忒弥斯协议》，以回应政府和私营公司对月球活动日益增长的兴趣。他们提出了第一套实用原则，旨在加强各国在探索月球、火星及其他地方时的安全和协调，并承诺各国：
五年后，唐纳德·J·特朗普总统的国家太空政策指示美国宇航局建立一个持续的月球前哨基地。通过这个月球基地，美国宇航局正在将《阿耳忒弥斯协议》的原则付诸实践，邀请所有签署方参与这一努力。
随着美国宇航局继续致力于在太空建立安全、和平和繁荣的未来，预计未来数月乃至数年将有更多国家签署《阿耳忒弥斯协议》。

item.title: 美国宇航局研究发现近地小行星实际上是彗星
item.description:
New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by precisely tracking its motion through space and using powerful observatories that image faint celestial objects.
This object has a dual personality: Past images hadn’t revealed obvious cometlike activity, suggesting it might be an asteroid, but its motion recently proved to be irregular like that of a comet. The scientists detailed their findings in a studypublishedin the journal Nature Astronomy.
The puzzle began on Aug. 28, 2025, when the object, provisionally known as the asteroid 1998 SH2, passed safely within 2 million miles (3 million kilometers) of our planet during its 4½-year orbit around the Sun. Researchers looking to observe 1998 SH2 with NASA’s Deep Space Network (DSN) planetary radar system had calculated its position using data from previous orbits and factored in the effects that the gravity of the Sun and planets would have on its path. But when 1998 SH2 didn’t show up where they expected, they realized that something unanticipated had been influencing the object’s motion.
By using optical astrometry to precisely measure the object’s position in the sky, the researchers were able to identify the cause.
“After we measured the nongravitational perturbations affecting the motion of 1998 SH2 and recognized they weren’t compatible with the object being an asteroid, we suspected the object could be an active comet,” said Davide Farnocchia, a navigation engineer with NASA’s Center for Near-Earth Object Studies at JPL and study lead.
Although 1998 SH2’s orbit around the Sun had been well-tracked from 1998 to 2016, the object had completed two solar orbits without additional observations by telescopes until the 2025 DSN attempts. Analyzing all observations collected since the object’s discovery in 1998, researchers determined the perturbations to 1998 SH2’s motion and hypothesized that the object may be generating a small thrust by venting gas into space, causing it to deviate from its predicted path.
This venting results from the Sun heating ice mixed with rocky material, turning the ice into a gas. With regular comets, this activity forms a trademark bright tail and coma — the gas and dust surrounding a comet’s nucleus. But when an object produces gas and dust in much smaller quantities, its tail and coma may not be detectable to most observatories.
The August 2025 close approach to Earth of 1998 SH2 provided the perfect opportunity for the paper’s authors to gather observational evidence of visible cometary activity. They reached out to astronomers at the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope, a 3.6-meter (12-foot) optical/infrared telescope near the summit of Mauna Kea, Hawaii, and the 1.5-meter (5-foot) European Southern Observatory’s Danish Telescope in La Silla, Chile, to observe. Astronomers at the powerful European Southern Observatory’s 8.2-meter (27-foot) Very Large Telescope on the Chilean mountain Cerro Paranal also tracked the object.
“The images we collected from these observatories showed a weak but clear tail, thus confirming that 1998 SH2 is, in fact, a comet,” said Olivier Hainaut, an astronomer with the European Southern Observatory and coauthor of the study. “That’s how science works — you form a hypothesis, and you set out to test it. This data is exactly what was needed to confirm our hypothesis that 1998 SH2 was a comet.”
As an outcome of the investigation, 1998 SH2 will receive an additional comet provisional designation, P/1998 SH2.
The research also sheds light on another, even more unusual, class of objects calleddark comets. Like 1998 SH2, dark comets exhibit significant irregularities, or perturbations, in their trajectory but lack other visible evidence of comet activity — there’s no coma, tail, or visible outgassing. These enigmatic objects fall into two distinct populations: larger ones with orbits similar to those of Jupiter-family comets (short period cometswith highly elliptical, or eccentric, orbits), and smaller ones that orbit closer to the Sun. Since the 2016 discovery of the first dark comet, about a dozen more have been identified.
The paper’s authors suggest that many of the larger dark comets, which have orbits like 1998 SH2’s, could turn out to be regular comets if astronomers get the right opportunity to observe them with powerful telescopes capable of imaging incredibly faint objects. And by analyzing the motion of all near-Earth objects using precision astrometry data, researchers may reveal more comets that were previously designated as asteroids if they exhibit cometlike nongravitational perturbations.
“This work shows the importance of continuously tracking near-Earth objects,” said Farnocchia. “Because of outgassing, the motion of comets is more significantly perturbed than that of asteroids. Detecting these perturbations can be an important diagnostic tool for planetary defense that will help u

item.title: 年轻星系团
item.description:
NASA 的詹姆斯·韦伯太空望远镜在 2026 年 7 月 3 日拍摄了一张年轻星系团 MACS J0553.4-3342 的图像，带我们穿越到了 44 亿年前。该星团由两个积极合并的子星团组成，质量大致相等。每个子星团都锚定在一个极其明亮和巨大的椭圆星系上，很容易识别为该场景中心的两个最亮点，周围有最大的发光光环。
图片来源：ESA/Webb、NASA 和 CSA、S. Fujimoto

item.title: 安大略省野火烟雾向东移动
item.description:
加拿大的野火使安大略省、魁北克省以及美国中西部和东北部的部分地区冒出滚滚浓烟。
加拿大 2026 年火灾季节缓慢启动后，到 6 月底，活动在干燥、温暖的条件下有所回升，并恢复到接近 25 年平均水平。据加拿大跨机构森林消防中心称，截至 7 月中旬，全国各地有近 850 处火灾正在燃烧。其中超过 180 个在安大略省燃烧。
这张 NOAA-21 图像于 2026 年 7 月 14 日下午获取，显示了安大略省大火冒出的滚滚浓烟。风将烟雾主要吹向东南方，覆盖该省南部的大部分地区以及魁北克省的部分地区以及美国中西部和东北部，将许多地区的天空染成灰色和黄色，将太阳染成橙色。
烟雾对空气质量的影响各不相同，很大程度上取决于海拔高度。在大气中烟雾浓度较高的地区，空气质量的影响可以忽略不计；当它靠近地面时，情况就会恶化。例如，据 AirNow 报道，多伦多的空气质量达到了不健康的水平。该省南部地区的人们也在应对热浪，这加剧了健康风险。
大部分烟雾来自安大略省西北部的火灾，7月13日至14日，该地区的火灾数量显着增加。 据新闻报道，火灾促使官员对该省该地区的几个社区发布了疏散令。
自今年年初以来，截至 7 月 14 日，加拿大各地的火灾已烧毁面积 190 万公顷（470 万英亩），仍远低于 2023 年和 2025 年极端火灾年份的季节总面积。本季节剩余时间的情况如何还有待观察。由来自美国、加拿大和墨西哥的荒地火灾专家编制的季节性火灾展望显示了 7 月、8 月和 9 月期间或多或少可能发生火灾的地区。
NASA 地球观测站图像，由 Lauren Dauphin 拍摄，使用来自 NASA EOSDIS LANCE、GIBS/Worldview 和联合极地卫星系统 (JPSS) 的 VIIRS 数据。凯瑟琳·汉森的故事。
当我们探索宇宙并发现更多关于我们的家园星球时，请随时了解 NASA 的最新内容。
在澳大利亚北领地易发生火灾的生态系统中，会进行规定的燃烧，以最大程度地减少稍后火灾的严重程度……
2026 年 3 月，美国大平原干燥、温暖和多风的天气导致了极端的火灾活动。
大火烧毁了150多平方英里，席卷了滑雪胜地的部分地区。
订阅地球观测站并将地球放入您的收件箱中。
美国宇航局地球观测站每天为您带来地球的深入故事和令人惊叹的图像。
开放访问 NASA 的地球科学数据档案

item.title: NASA 使用小型飞机加速飞行创新
item.description:
Testing new aerospace concepts in flight remains one of NASA’s most effective ways to advance knowledge and reduce risk.
The Dale Reed Subscale Flight Research Laboratory at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, supports this mission by using small, remotely piloted and autonomous aircraft as cost‑effective platforms to mature innovative ideas, accelerate learning, and enable smoother transitions to full‑scale flight.
When experiments require a flight platform, several NASA remotely piloted aircraft are available: the Alta‑X quadrotor; the Dryden Remotely Operated Integrated Drone (DROID) with its 10‑foot wingspan; and the Multi‑Use Cub, a 14‑foot‑span fixed‑wing aircraft with an expandable payload capacity for flight experiments. For electric vertical takeoff and landing testing, the HQ‑90 quadrotor provides an additional option.
Once aircraft and experiments are cleared for operations, laboratory pilots support the mission, including ground operations and flight activities.
Each staff member serves as an experienced and certified subscale aircraft pilot and is prepared to fly unique one-of-a-kind or modified commercial aircraft wherever the mission requires.
NASA’sFireSenseproject conducted flights in the Geneva State Forest, located about 100 miles south of Montgomery, Alabama. NASA Armstrong flight research staff integrated the instrument onto an Alta-X drone and tested the system before deployment. Two team members then transported the drone and sensor to the forest, prepared the vehicle for flight, and operated it during the mission. The NASA sensor was flown on the drone to demonstrate how remotely piloted aircraft can gather localized weather data that influences smoke movement and fire behavior. This information may help operational agencies improve wildfire decision-making and better allocate firefighters and resources.
Other missions occur closer to NASA Armstrong, such as the Enhancing Parachutes by Instrumenting the Canopy (EPIC) project. EPIC involved air‑launching a capsule containing a parachute and flexible sensor from the Alta‑X. Laboratory staff piloted the flights, supported flight operations, and worked with the EPIC team to design and integrate the parachute‑drop mechanism and safety system into the aircraft.
These tests demonstrated that a flexible sensor could help researchers study supersonic parachutes. Continuation of this work can help fill gaps in computer models, making supersonic parachutes safer and more reliable for delivering science instruments and payloads to Mars.
The Dale Reed Subscale Flight Research Laboratory uses rapid design and testing capabilities to help small aircraft fly big ideas. These concepts could lead to future breakthroughs that support NASA’s missions across aeronautics, science, and exploration.
For decades, NASA and its partners have advancedAutomatic Collision Avoidance Technology. The research demonstrated an autopilot could detect and recover from an imminent ground collision – a capability now helping save lives in high‑performance U.S. military jets. NASA Armstrong had key roles in that work and developed a simplified version, theAutomatic Ground Collision Avoidance System, which was installed on the DROID for testing.
The system demonstrated on the DROID — developed to assist general aviation pilots as well as remotely piloted and autonomous aircraft — performed well and led to further research toward a version that provides alerts and steering cues. The NASA Armstrong Technology Transfer Office is working to license the technology for U.S. businesses to develop the system as a commercial product.
ThePrandtl‑D(Preliminary Research Aerodynamic Design to Lower Drag) flying‑wing glider was also designed, fabricated, and flown at NASA Armstrong. Researchers found that its twisted wing design could reduce drag and generate thrust at the wingtips, advancing concepts that may support greater fuel economy for future aircraft. The original Prandtl‑D is now part of the Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum collection in Washington, and the Prandtl-D3 is at the California Science Center in Los Angeles. Researchers continue developing the next generation of the design in the laboratory.
A wide range of capabilities in the laboratory help transform promising concepts into flight-ready test structures. These include rapid prototyping using traditional and advanced 3D manufacturing techniques, as well as composite and conventional fabrication processes. The team of engineers and technicians also provides custom component design and specialized fabrication to meet unique research needs.
The laboratory supports electrical and mechanical design, hardware and software integration, and the safety and flight-readiness processes required for successful missions. Additional technical facilities, such as theExperimental Fabrication Branchand the Environmental Laboratory at NASA Armstrong, further enhance these capabilities. T

item.title: 美国宇航局对原始陨石的研究为古代小行星的故事增添了色彩
item.description:
A meteorite recovered immediately upon its fall to Earth on July 16, 2024, is helping NASA scientists uncover new clues about ancient water, the chemical evolution of primitive asteroids, and the ingredients that may have helped make life possible throughout the early solar system.
This rapid recovery began when an amateur astronomer in New Jersey quickly recognized that a newly fallen meteorite had landed on his property. Recognizing its scientific value and wearing protective gloves, he collected the fragments and stored them in aluminum foil and glass containers, which preserved delicate minerals and organic compounds that are often altered by moisture, weather, and contamination.
As the meteorite fell to Earth, cameras across New Jersey captured its fiery passage through the atmosphere. Scientists used these observations to reconstruct the fireball's trajectory and, after recovering the meteorite, combined this data with laboratory analyses to determine where in the solar system the rock most likely originated. In a study published Wednesday in the journalScience Advances, researchers found evidence that ancient salty water altered minerals within the meteorite's parent asteroid, preserving unique minerals and a rich inventory of organic compounds.
"When we have both a documented fireball and a quick recovery of its meteorite, we can learn not only what the rock is made of, but where it came from in the asteroid belt," said Peter Jenniskens, meteor astronomer at both NASA's Ames Research Center in California's Silicon Valley and the SETI Institute, and lead author of the study.
Named for the township where it was recovered, the Hillsborough meteorite belongs to a class of carbon-rich meteorites known as CM carbonaceous chondrites. These primitive rocks preserve some of the oldest materials in the solar system, recording the chemical processes that shaped asteroids more than 4.5 billion years ago.
While examining the unusually pristine meteorite, researchers found a mosaic of tiny broken-up rocks and noticed that some contained unusually high concentrations of sodium — an unexpected finding for this type of meteorite. The surprising signal prompted a closer investigation using powerful electron microscopes that allowed scientists to examine the meteorite from the millimeter scale down to individual atoms. By combining observations across multiple scales, researchers reconstructed the history of the minerals and the fluids that once flowed through them.
These analyses revealed microscopic fractures filled with sodium-rich material left behind by ancient brines. Unlike pure water, brines contain dissolved salts that allow them to transport elements and chemically alter the rocks they move through. In the case of the Hillsborough sample, those ancient fluids altered the asteroid's minerals and left behind chemical evidence that remained preserved for billions of years.
Scientists were also able to detect fragile sodium-carbonate salts that normally react with moisture in Earth's atmosphere before they can be studied. Jangmi Han, a paper co-author and mineralogist at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, identified evidence of ancient brines preserved within microscopic fractures. Similar salts were identified in samples returned from the asteroids Bennu and Ryugu by NASA'sOSIRIS-RExmission and JAXA's (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency)Hayabusa2mission. However,Hillsborough marks the first time the salts have been identified in a CM carbonaceous chondrite meteorite, offering a new glimpse into the surfaces of the primitive asteroids that produced these meteorites.
Together, these findings suggest that ancient, salt-rich brines were more widespread amongprimitive asteroidsthan previously recognized, and provide scientists with new opportunities to compare how water altered different asteroid bodies across the early solar system.
"The chips of the most salt-rich bits of this meteorite are quite comparable to the samples returned by the Hayabusa2 and OSIRIS-REx missions," said Mike Zolensky, a meteorite researcher at NASA Johnson and co-author of the study. "They're not identical. They're different in some very interesting ways, but they've seen very similar processes."
Scientists expected Hillsborough to contain a rich suite of organic compounds because it is a CM carbonaceous chondrite. What made the meteorite exceptional was how quickly it was recovered, allowing researchers to study those compounds before prolonged exposure to Earth's environment could contaminate the sample.
"One of the big surprises for me when we analyzed a small chip of the Hillsborough meteorite was the complexity of amino acids and other organic compounds," said Danny Glavin, senior scientist in the Astrobiology Analytical Laboratory at NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, and co-author of the study.
Its diversity of amino acids and other organic compounds is, comparable to the Murchison meteorite,
